---
title: "Data Warehouses and Data Lakes Review"
subtitle: "Warehouse Structure, Lake Optionality, Lakehouse Tables, Metadata, Governance, and Workload Fit"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Architecture objective

Define whether each workload needs warehouse-style curated analytical state, lake-style raw optionality, or lakehouse-style convergence.

## Reproducible example

```{python}
assets = [
    {"asset": "fact_sales", "zone": "warehouse_fact", "schema": "schema_on_write"},
    {"asset": "raw_clickstream_events", "zone": "raw_lake", "schema": "schema_on_read"},
]
print(assets)
```

## Governance review

Check metadata, lineage, ownership, sensitivity classification, access policy, lifecycle status, and certification status.

## Performance review

Compare query latency, scan efficiency, storage cost, compute cost, cache hit rate, and cost status.

## Appendix

Attach data assets, lake zones, dimensional models, governance controls, cost/performance metrics, lakehouse features, and workload requirements.
